title: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rachael Rollins is running for Suffolk County District Attorney after resigning from her U.S. Attorney position in 2023.
Rollins' resignation followed a DOJ probe into ethical breaches involving meddling in the DA race and leaking sensitive information.
She faces current DA Kevin Hayden and attorney Linda Champion in the Suffolk DA race as of April 2026.

title: Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelmsford firefighter Nick Spinale fell 40 feet during a training exercise, sustaining life-threatening injuries.
Spinale, 28, is in intensive care at UMass Memorial Medical Center, requiring multiple surgeries.
Chelmsford Firefighters' Union calls for community support, highlighting emotional response from fellow firefighters.

title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox's new second-base celebration is inspired by a TikTok dance video of outfielder Roman Anthony from high school.
Manager Alex Cora confirmed the dance moves originated from a popular TikTok choreography set to Mariah Carey's 'Obsessed.'
Roman Anthony, batting .217, contributes to Red Sox's gradual improvement, culminating in their first series win against the Brewers.

title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kevin Scott Kelley charged with fraud and larceny related to false overtime claims during tenure as Adams police chief.
Kelley allegedly submitted fraudulent claims for $19,123.15 in overtime under a MassDOT grant between January 2024 and September 2025.
Kelley was placed on paid leave in September 2025 and no longer served as chief by December 2025.

title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions demand Chief Charles Gray's removal after over 90% vote no confidence in his leadership.
Unions cite leadership failures, including lack of body-worn cameras, impacting the acquittal of former officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons.
Gray criticized for not adopting a comprehensive shooting policy; a 2020 management review highlighted similar leadership concerns.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics are reportedly interested in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo as Milwaukee Bucks face team challenges.
Celtics' current roster expenses pose financial challenges to acquiring Antetokounmpo due to luxury tax implications.
Giannis Antetokounmpo is under contract until 2028 with a player option, facing possible free agency after next season.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox claimed their first series win of 2026 with a 5-0 victory over the Brewers.
Sonny Gray showcased strong pitching, delivering 6 1/3 scoreless innings, reducing his ERA to 2.76.
Rookie Tyler Samaniego impressed with three strikeouts in his MLB debut during the eighth inning.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget for Boston's fiscal year 2027, with a 2.1% spending increase.
The budget addresses financial challenges due to rising costs, inflation, and slowing revenues, while maintaining staffing and services.
Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit; measures include delaying hiring and spending freezes to close the gap.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the Patriots Hall of Fame.
Gronkowski is a five-time Pro Bowler and all-time leader in touchdowns for the Patriots.
Vinatieri holds NFL records for most points and made field goals, while Mankins started 130 games for the Patriots.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract to bolster their roster for the playoffs.
Hagens, 19, previously played for the Providence Bruins with a power-play goal and three assists in six games.
Hagens is expected to join the Bruins' lineup and improve their depth and power-play unit before his potential NHL debut.
